zh.Wethersfield, Essex, England: 英國艾可維瑟斯費爾德
zh.Great Hallingbury, Essex, England: 英國艾可格亞特 哈林波利
zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan de Cornhill於1180年出生在英國英格蘭時，她的父親Henry de Cornhill, Sheriff of London45歲，母親Alice De Curci23歲。她與Hugh de Neville結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1224年12月在英國享年44歲。

zh-hans.Wethersfield, Essex, England: 英国艾可維瑟斯費爾德
zh-hans.Great Hallingbury, Essex, England: 英国艾可格亞特 哈林波利
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan de Cornhill于1180年出生在英国英格兰时，她的父亲Henry de Cornhill, Sheriff of London45岁，母亲Alice De Curci23岁。她与Hugh de Neville结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1224年12月在英国享年44岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Cornhill
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1  English: locative name in SE England mainly from Cornhill in London but elsewhere alternatively from Cornhill (Northumb) or one of the many minor places so called. It has often become   Cornell   and has sometimes been confused with   Cornwall  , and   Cornwell  , as in the case of Thomas Cornell, 1722 in   Parish Registers   (Horringer, Suffolk), who is also called Cornwall (1736), Cornwell (1740) and Cornhill (1766).
2  English:  see  Cornall  .
3  English:  see  Cornwall  .

variants.name: Cornwall
meanings: English:

habitational name from the county of Cornwall, which is named with the Old English tribal name Cornwealas. This is from Kernow (the term that the Cornish used to refer to themselves, a word of uncertain etymology, perhaps connected with a Celtic element meaning ‘horn, headland’), + Old English wealas ‘strangers, foreigners’, the term used by the Anglo-Saxons for British-speaking people.
variant of   Cornwell  .

variants.name: Parish Registers
meanings: 1 English: relationship name, nickname from the Middle English personal name * Baioc, a diminutive of the personal name Bay . Redmonds notes Arthur Bake, Baick, or Bayock, 1621–32 in Parish Registers (Hampsthwaite, WR Yorks). Some bearers may belong with (2).
2  English: variant of   Back  .

variants.name: Cornwell
meanings: English:
habitational name from Cornwell in Oxfordshire, named from Old English corn, a metathesized form of cron, cran ‘crane’ + well(a) ‘spring, stream’.

variant of Cornwall or Cornhill; in southeastern England mainly a habitational name from Cornhill in London but elsewhere alternatively from Cornhill (Northumberland) or one of the many minor places so called, often becoming Cornell .

variants.name: Cornell
meanings: English: shortened form of Cornwell , Cornwall , or of Cornhill, a habitational name from a place in Northumberland named Cornhill, from Old English corn, a metathesized form of cron, cran ‘crane’ + halh ‘nook, recess’; or from Cornhill in London, a medieval grain exchange, named with Old English corn ‘corn, grain’ + hyll ‘hill’, or from some other place elsewhere similarly named.
English: variant of   Corney  .
English: possibly also a nickname from French corneille ‘rook, crow’, probably denoting a chatterer or someone with dark hair or a dark complexion.

variants.name: Cornall
meanings: 1 English: the Lancs name is a variant of Corney , where -all and -ell are hypercorrect pronunciations of Cornah, Corner, and Cornoe, late pronunciations of Corney.

2 English: the Devon name is a variant of Cornwall . Here also probably belongs Gurnall, as in Joan Gurnall, 1586 in IGI (Crediton, Devon), and William Gurnall, 1650 in IGI (Lavenham, Suffolk). Compare Corney .

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: Greek: the Lord gave graciously; whom Jehovah gave; whom Jehovah bestowed.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan de Cornhill was born in 1180, in England, United Kingdom, her father, Henry de Cornhill, Sheriff of London, was 45 and her mother, Alice De Curci, was 23. She married Hugh de Neville. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. She died in December 1224, in England, at the age of 44.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan de Cornhill est née en 1180, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Henry de Cornhill, Sheriff of London, avait 45 ans et sa mère, Alice De Curci, avait 23 ans. Elle a épousé Hugh de Neville. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en décembre 1224, à Angleterre, à l’âge de 44 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan de Cornhill 1180 in England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Henry de Cornhill, Sheriff of London 45 und ihre Mutter Alice De Curci 23. Sie hat Hugh de Neville geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb im Dezember 1224 im Alter von 44 Jahren in England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Cornhill è nata il nel 1180 nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Henry de Cornhill, Sheriff of London, aveva 45 anni e sua madre, Alice De Curci, aveva 23 anni. Lei sposato Hugh de Neville. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel dicembre 1224, nel England, all'età di 44 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan de Cornhillが1180年にイギリスイギリスで生まれた時，父Henry de Cornhill, Sheriff of Londonは45歳，母Alice De Curciは23歳であった。Hugh de Nevilleと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1224年12月にイギリスで44歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan de Cornhill(이)가 1180년 영국 잉글랜드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Henry de Cornhill, Sheriff of London(은)는 45세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alice De Curci(은)는 23세였다. 그녀는 Hugh de Neville(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1224년 12월 영국에서 44세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Cornhill nasceu em 1180, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Henry de Cornhill, Sheriff of London, tinha 45 anos e sua mãe, Alice De Curci, tinha 23 anos. Ela casou-se com Hugh de Neville. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em dezembro de 1224, em Inglaterra, com 44 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan de Cornhill родилась в 1180 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Henry de Cornhill, Sheriff of London было 45 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice De Curci, 23. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Hugh de Neville. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла декабрь 1224 г. в местности Англия в возрасте 44 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan de Cornhill nació en 1180, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Henry de Cornhill, Sheriff of London, tenía 45 años y su mamá, Alice De Curci, tenía 23 años. Se casó con Hugh de Neville. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en diciembre de 1224, en Inglaterra, a la edad de 44 años.
